name: angr (Binary Analysis Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 2-Clause); UC Santa Barbara SecLab

performance.notes: Symbolic execution is computationally expensive; path explosion common; hours for complex binaries

agent_readiness.notes: Binary analysis and symbolic execution framework. Control flow graph recovery, symbolic execution (concolic testing), taint analysis, binary lifting, constraint solving (Z3 integration). Supports x86/x64, ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, etc. Used for: CTF challenges, vulnerability research, firmware analysis, automated exploit generation. Steep learning curve — binary analysis expertise required. Core framework in academic security research.

gotchas: Path explosion makes symbolic execution impractical for large binaries
Results can be unsound if analysis assumptions are wrong
best_when: Binary CTF challenges requiring symbolic execution
Binary lifting and control flow analysis
summary: angr is a binary analysis and symbolic execution framework for security research. Control flow graph recovery, symbolic execution, taint analysis, Z3 integration. Supports multiple architectures (x86, ARM, MIPS). Used for CTF challenges, firmware analysis, and vulnerability research. Very steep learning curve and computationally expensive (path explosion). Essential for advanced binary security research.
